items.title: Naive Bayes factorization given class y
items.mermaid:
flowchart TB
  y["Class label y"]
  joint["Joint P(x|y)"]
  x1["P(x1|y)"]
  x2["P(x2|y)"]
  xd["P(xd|y)"]
  prod["Product: P(x1|y) * ... * P(xd|y)"]
  y --> joint
  joint --> prod
  x1 --> prod
  x2 --> prod
  xd --> prod
items.caption: Conditional independence replaces the high-dimensional joint P(x|y) with a product of per-feature terms.

items.title: Bayes classification pipeline
items.mermaid:
flowchart LR
  prior["P(y) prior"]
  like["Product of P(xi|y)"]
  num["P(y) * P(x|y)"]
  post["P(y|x) posterior"]
  pred["argmax_y score"]
  prior --> num
  like --> num
  num --> post
  post --> pred
items.caption: Naive Bayes combines the class prior with the factored likelihood; argmax over y yields the predicted label.

items.title: Conditional vs marginal independence
items.mermaid:
flowchart TB
  marginal["Marginal: x1 and x2 may correlate"]
  givenY["Given y fixed: assume x1 perp x2"]
  note["Naive Bayes uses conditional independence only"]
  marginal --> givenY
  givenY --> note
items.caption: Features can correlate overall; the naive assumption applies only after conditioning on the class label.
